sysname: SAGE — Scaffolded AI Guidance for Engagement

S.name: System under evaluation
S.description: A pedagogical tutor that teaches AI-agent literacy via a practice → scaffolded feedback → closing reflection loop. Implemented on the Anthropic SDK; decomposed into 18 skills under .claude/skills/. Runtime surfaces: Streamlit web app (sage-ui) and terminal CLI.
in_scope: Question discipline (≤ 1 question mark per SAGE turn)
Front-loading discipline (≤ 4 sentences before first question)
Answer-first behaviour on direct learner questions
out_of_scope: Factual accuracy of pedagogical content delivered by SAGE
Long-horizon learner outcomes (learning retention, behaviour change)

P.name: Population / participants
P.intrinsic: Three persona-driven simulated learners: novice-curious (yes/no questions), skeptical-engineer (factual questions), fatigued-returner (short messages, wraps early). Plus 7 hand-authored gold transcripts (4 positive, 3 negative cases).
P.extrinsic: ≥ 3 university students outside the project team, recruited for a 15-minute session each.

H.name: How it was evaluated

intrinsic_metric_1.name: Front-Loading Discipline
intrinsic_metric_1.definition: Per SAGE turn: question_marks ≤ 1 AND sentences_before_first_question_mark ≤ 4. Pass iff both sub-checks pass.

intrinsic_metric_2.name: Answer-First Adherence
intrinsic_metric_2.stage_1: Haiku 4.5 classifies preceding learner message as yes_no | factual | open | not_a_question. Only yes_no and factual qualify.
intrinsic_metric_2.stage_2: Sonnet 4.6 grades the tutor reply as answered_first | answered_and_followed_up | redirected_without_answer | non_answer. Pass = first two.

H.extrinsic: Task-based user study; Likert satisfaction (1–5) + per-turn issue tagging (multi-question, didn't-answer-my-question). Transcripts exported from the Streamlit app feed back into the intrinsic harness via --exports-only.

discriminative_validity.interpretation: Rule-based metric correctly separates positive (well-crafted) from negative (deliberately-flawed) authored transcripts.

R.name: Risks and threats to validity
items: Small persona corpus: 19 simulated SAGE turns per agent version produces fragile per-persona percentages.
Conversation-flow divergence: each agent version drives the persona LLM into a different applicable-turn set, confounding rate comparison on Answer-First.
Single LLM judge not cross-validated against human labellers; potential model-family bias on the grader.
Front-Loading is a strict rule; user study (extrinsic) will tell us whether the rule is too strict (i.e. multi-question turns are tolerable in some contexts).

Empirical_strength.rationale: Two independent metrics (rule-based + LLM-judge); apples-to-apples comparison across two agent versions via the same persona prompts; deterministic + LLM-judge mix. Weaknesses: small corpus, no human-judge alignment study, single-judge ensemble.
